decision.question: Distinguish repo layout from concurrency issues before blaming the parent-root git structure.
decision.chosen: Determined the layout was intentional and safe; the real bug was overlapping mutating git operations against the same top-level index.
decision.rationale: The git index lock kept recurring, and the question was whether the top-level .git one folder above SPAWN was inherently wrong.

outcome.result: Derived steering backfill captured from the March 28 collaboration thread.
outcome.lesson: Shared git roots are fine when writes are serialized.
outcome.follow_up: Use this correction family to weight future orchestration, frontend, and verifier decisions more heavily.
